posts.hook: 1 in 3 adults has astigmatism. Most don't know it.
posts.caption:
Astigmatism causes blurry vision at ALL distances — not just far away.

And it's the most under-diagnosed refractive error.

Symptoms:
- Halos around lights at night
- Eyestrain after 30 minutes of reading
- Headaches after screen use
- Squinting to see clearly

Free astigmatism awareness test: axeo.vision

#Astigmatism #EyeHealth #VisionTest #Axeo #HealthTok #EyeCare

posts.hook: What astigmatism actually does to your vision — animated.
posts.caption:
Astigmatism = your cornea is oval-shaped instead of round.

Result: light focuses in two places instead of one.

This causes:
- Blur at ALL distances (unlike myopia — only far)
- Distortion, not just fuzziness
- Worse at night (pupils dilate = more distortion)

Tested with: Jackson Cross Cylinder method.

Or free: axeo.vision

#Astigmatism #Optometry #Axeo #EyeHealth #HealthTok #VisionScience
posts.visual_notes: Animated diagram of cornea shape. Light ray animation showing double focal point. Simulation of astigmatic vs normal vision on text/lights.

posts.hook: We filmed every vision test on Axeo. All 4. In 30 seconds.
posts.caption:
Axeo includes 4 complete vision tests — all free, no sign-up:

1. Snellen acuity (20/20 baseline)
2. Color vision (Ishihara-style)
3. Astigmatism pattern test
4. Contrast sensitivity

Takes under 5 minutes total.

Axeo stores your baseline so you can track changes over time.

Free at axeo.vision

posts.hook: Why night driving feels impossible — and it's not your glasses.
posts.caption:
Night driving difficulty is a classic astigmatism symptom.

Here's why:
- Pupils dilate in the dark
- More of the irregular cornea is exposed
- Streetlights, headlights → starburst halos
- Oncoming lights cause glare and scatter

Most people blame their prescription. The issue is corneal shape.

Astigmatism test: axeo.vision

#NightDriving #Astigmatism #EyeHealth #Axeo #Optometrist #VisionTest #EyeCare

posts.hook: Astigmatism: the vision problem 1 in 3 people have — and most don't know it.
posts.caption:
Astigmatism affects ~33% of adults.

Unlike myopia (blurry far) or hyperopia (blurry near) — astigmatism blurs at ALL distances.

Symptoms: halos around lights, eyestrain, headaches.
Often dismissed as 'just tired eyes'.

Free awareness test: axeo.vision
posts.visual_notes: Infographic: cornea shape diagram (round vs oval) + symptom list. Teal/dark Axeo brand colors.

posts.hook: How to know if you have astigmatism without going to an optometrist (free self-test methods)
posts.caption:
r/EyeCare or r/optometry

Astigmatism is remarkably common (~33% of adults) and often self-detectable.

Three at-home methods:

**1. The wheel/sunburst test**
Look at a starburst pattern. If some lines look darker or sharper than others in one direction, you likely have astigmatism.

**2. The vertical/horizontal line test**
Look at a grid. If horizontal lines appear clearer than vertical (or vice versa), classic astigmatic sign.

**3. Cover test at night**
Cover one eye in the dark and look at a distant streetlight. Starburst halo = strong indicator.

These don't replace a clinical exam but give a strong indication. Free web-based version at axeo.vision (no sign-up).

Happy to answer questions.
